lines.text: Four years have passed, and I stand before you once more. Not as a Gladiator, but as a witness to the start of this grand event.
You all know well that Gladiatorial combat is the lifeblood of our ancient and mighty city.
"Seize power, and fight for victory."
Once, we fought to survive on the seas. That same instinct courses through our veins today. The instinct to face fate head-on and defy it.
Remember, those who cling to glory without the strength to defend it will see it slip through their fingers.
Warriors! By the eternal sun, may your courage honor Septimont and prove your loyalty to its spirit!
I look forward to the rise of new heroes in this Agon.
Let the Agon begin.
Speak plainly, Gladiator.
Julia Silva, you know the weight of your actions.
I know your family once stood as the cornerstone of this city. Its pioneers, its founders, its protectors.
For centuries, the Silvas fought bravely in the hunting grounds, defending Septimont from Tacet Discords.
But today, you dishonor their legacy. Do not disgrace them further, Julia.
Leave Septimont. Do not ever return.
The day of the final is here.
Arsinosa watches, and her significance is known to every Septimontian. It is etched in chronicles and sung in legends.
If you seek the glory of Arsinosa's blessing, then fight with everything you have!
Show me if any of you four possess the strength to earn the Guardian's recognition.
Brilliant performance, warriors.
Remember, this is more than just a title. It is a testament to your own strength and wisdom in overcoming your opponents. You've earned the right to claim it.
This medal once belonged to a single champion, the last survivor standing in the ring.
I divided it into two to remind you that...
Just as we fight alongside Echoes, the glory of Septimont is upheld by heroes who support one another to remain steadfast.
Fighting alone is commendable. However, true strength lies in protecting those beside you.
The High Tide draws near. I want all warriors heading to the hunting grounds to remember this.
You've done incredibly well.
Your strength comes from deep in your heart, unshakable and selfless. It shines above Septimont and reminds everyone what being a hero truly means.
Fighting by your side has been an honor. By the eternal blazing sun, your name will be remembered in Septimont forever.
This world will never forget what you've done for us, {PlayerName}.
Everything is in place.
It will take the bait.
Now is not the moment
Let it flee. The path it takes leads deeper into the trap we laid
Impressive!
I expected a challenge, but your performance far exceeds what I had imagined.
Just as I thought, watching from the Colosseum's balcony is no way to understand a warrior's true spirit.
Only by crossing blades can I feel the will to fight pulsing through your veins.
Come. Step closer.
Laureate of Ragunna. Champion of Septimont. Favored Gladiator of <ano=Lioness of Glory>Arsinosa</ano>—by the honor of these titles, it is only right you stand by my side.
I, Augusta, Ephor of Septimont, offer you my highest esteem.
Hehe. Simple and efficient, no need for pleasantries. Here, we let our blades do the talking. The Septimont way.
I have no doubt that you and I will have plenty of opportunities to get acquainted. But that's not why I called you here today...
On my behalf, and that of the Ephor's Palace, I extend my deepest apologies for failing to keep our appointment.
I hope this delay hasn't dampened your enthusiasm for the hunt.
Haha. Tolerant and strong. Just as I've heard. Virtues of a true hero.
Well, then. Seeing that your interest still holds. Shall we discuss it?
The hunt—in Septimont's long fight for survival, it holds profound significance...
It is both honor and duty. For a hundred years, countless Gladiators have taken up the hunt and met their end in glory.
It's no exaggeration to say that the blood of Gladiators still burns in every inch of the crimson Sanguis Plateaus.
Our ancestors built the defenses that line those grounds. To this day, they stand as the frontline of Septimont's resistance against the Dark Tide.

You've surely noticed the lingering Blightcloud that hovers over the Sanguis Plateaus. Since the first Dark Tide outbreak in Rinascita, that place has become a breeding ground for its <te href=750002>horrors</te>.
As you've no doubt realized, our prey are Tacet Discords born from the Dark Tide. Only those who desecrate Septimont fall beneath the Gladiators' blades.
The High Tide is upon us... and it will be a storm like no other.
My hunting party requires unstoppable warriors. Sacrifices are inevitable, but I believe you will be a banner that stands unbroken, {PlayerName}.
I share your sentiment. Septimont takes pride in its many remarkable Gladiators. But you, you are exceptional.
A grand hunt awaits us, and my warriors and I long to do battle alongside a fighter such as yourself.
Of course, the will of the extraordinary deserves to be heard.
I'd like to know your thoughts on the matter, {PlayerName}.
A straightforward answer. I like it.
As for this "goal" of yours...
Ah, yes, the Journeying Paradise. I've heard that laughable name before. A pity the hunt was made to shatter that little fantasy.
As for the Sentinel's Relic...
Go to the <te href=750003>Earthrend Wedge</te>. It's where the Sentinel Imperator first descended upon the Sanguis Plateaus and is the "epicenter" of every hunt to this day.
However, due to its significance, not even Septimont's highest officials are permitted near it.
But if that is what you wish... say no more. I will make it happen.
The trust I place in you stems from your recognition of my character. I admire your honesty, and I will honor it to the fullest.
Only those bound by trust can go the distance together.
I look forward to the hunt ahead. It honors me to journey by your side.
Patience, my friend, is the first step to becoming a hunter.
When the storm rages in the skies southeast of Septimont, I will await you at <te href=750004>Tempest Cliffs</te>.
Come. Join us, {PlayerName}.
The horns signal our departure.
Precisely. These cliffs mark the opening of our campaign.
Hahaha! Take courage, my friend.
The path to glory will reveal itself under the feet of the brave.
It's rare to see Iuno hold back like that. You must be someone... different to her.
Perhaps... We do share some common traits.
Hehe. Don't let her unruly side fool you. She is as dependable as they come.
A Corrosaurus.
Avidius was correct. It is a Tacet Discord born from the Dark Tide.
What he didn't have the chance to tell you is that it is the deadliest Corrosaurus of them all—we call it "Tyrannos." A vanguard of the Dark Tide that rides its surge to reach our lands.
The Threnodian's attempt to plague Septimont never came to fruition. But do not mistake that for victory. Our enemy will not bow so easily.
The Dark Tide, immortal and indestructible, carries out the will of its creator—the Threnodian Leviathan.
Only the Earthrend Wedge that bears Sentinel Imperator's power can sever the link between the Dark Tide and the Threnodian, burying the calamity deep beneath the Sanguis Plateaus.
The "hunt" is how we disrupt the Dark Tide's frequencies by capturing and eliminating its creatures.
However, the Earthrend Wedge's energy range is limited. Our mission is to deliver a true death to our prey.
Yes. And it must be done within <te href=750006>Maroonwood</te>.
This confrontation with Tyrannos was expected, but it's not a fight anyone is eager to face...
That beast is well aware of our plan. It's no mere prey. In many ways, it's a cunning hunter itself.
Now we wait. We wait for it to enter Maroonwood.
The real hunt begins when the beast's hunger drives it to the wilds.
Looks like I'll be occupied for a while.
Relax. Take a walk around camp. The <te href=750007>Hornett</te>'s blare will sound our next departure.
It's a sound that can be heard across the <te href=750008>canyons</te>. You won't miss it.
Over there.
Behind that cliff is the Earthrend Wedge. A relic of Sentinel Imperator as ancient as these lands.
The destination of your journey. My promise to you. Our shared goal.
An <te href=750013>Anchor Stone</te> made from Tacetite formed around the Earthrender.
It contains a fraction of the Earthrender's power. It offers no defense capabilities, but it can at least serve as a temporary measure to hold off the Dark Tide or its creations.

We need to deploy three of these in pre-determined locations across Maroonwood to generate a force field. This will drive the Corrosaurus to the <te href=750015>Asphodel Barrens</te> in the lower levels.
The field blooming with Asphodels is where the hunting party can safely reach the lower levels. Judging from the height, it's close to the Earthrender's base.
In that field of glory, we will grant Tyrannos the death it deserves.
Tyrannos's calling card as the vanguard of the Dark Tide. Our prey isn't exactly known for playing by the rules.
Maroonwood lies on the other side of these cliffs. That beast has reasoned we're likely to take to the skies on Griffrexes and has laid an ambush, waiting to drag us down into the abyss.
...A Corrosaurus that knows how to set a trap, waiting for its hunters to walk right into it... Heh. Quite an interesting opponent.
Gladiators, march with me to the hunting grounds.
Let the Sacred Flames ignite your resolve! The enemies of Septimont shall bear witness to our might!
Let us be on our way.
Avidius, descendant of the Hero of Heroes, Atilius. Decorated for the four crossings of Manes Deep.
I see the desire in you. Your desire for power.
Yet if you wish to be my opponent, then this level of resolve is far from enough.
Step forward. Stand next to me and pursue your desires without hesitation.
Let your ambitions burn even hotter.
Good. Ensure timely reports on any new developments.
I've made arrangements for the war effort. Strategically, we must always brace ourselves for the worst.
Communications with the Black Shores are also underway.

The Ephor's Palace has previously established a connection with a Consultant in the Rinascita region. We've reached an agreement granting the Black Shores permission to access Septimont's Resonance Nexus. They'll be able to monitor and collect data on the Dark Tide during the hunt.
In return, should any complications arise, the Black Shores are to fulfill their side of the agreement and provide us with the necessary technical support.
That's right. Her name is Buling. An interesting young lady. Left quite an impression on me.
Of course. I'll leave it to you then.
You mean...
You are right on the mark.
One way or another, we must find the Sovereign and stop it before it absorbs any more frequencies... This might be our only option for now.
Murmurstown...
Understood. I will stop it in its tracks.
Very well. You have sworn your allegiance to Septimont with your resolve. Your bravery is witnessed not only by me, but by the fallen heroes lying in Capitoline Hill. They take pride in your courage.
If you wish to fight together, then so be it. I will afford your fervor its due respect.
Come. Take up your blades—For Septimont. For the name of heroes eternal.
How are you holding up?
A ruler assumes their place... by accomplishing the impossible for their subjects.
It isn't safe here. I'll lead a team and escort everyone to a temporary camp not far from here. But before that, I must ensure no one is left behind.
You go on ahead, {PlayerName}. I am entrusting Iuno to your care.
Fool. I already said Ragunna's "Divinity" didn't get a seat on the ship
A hard-won battle... wasn't it?
Yes...
It's all thanks to you, {PlayerName}—Septimont's Hero of Heroes.
Extraordinary power? That's a bit much. I know my limits.
But… I do think I've earned that title, and it's one we could share together.
Besides, the Temple never said there could only be one Hero of Heroes.
Hehe, it wasn't a pleasant experience. But in a strange way… it was refreshing.
If you want to hear the full story, I'll tell you another time.
But, back inside the Dark Tide, I think the lost frequencies were always guiding me, shaping who I was becoming.
Even trapped in that abyss of despair, those fallen heroes never gave up. They lit a faint, warm light in the dark for me.
And you, along with everyone who stood by me… without you, we couldn't have won this battle.
Perhaps the title of "ruler" is too narrow and too hollow to define the true spirit of humankind.
A civilization's glory is forged by generations of brave souls. It was never meant to be one person's medal of honor.
Because my warriors and I wanted to fight beside someone like you. That hasn't changed.
But… I will be honest. Part of it was because of your "unwritten fate."

I'd been preparing for this hunt for a long time. At first, all we had were a few vague glimpses from the Priestesses and a handful of prophetic murals. No one knew when or how the disaster would strike.
And then you showed up. You've surely seen the unfinished mural in the Temple. The blank wall and the unwritten fate...
So I took a gamble, believing you were the key to this hunt.
I couldn't explain it back then, not when I wasn't certain myself.
But in the end, we realized what that "blank" represents. And my gamble paid off.
The one who deserves to be a Hero of Heroes, with power over the Earthrend Wedge...
Thank you for your trust, {PlayerName}.
Now, it's time for me to honor my promise.
This is the end of our journey... the Earthrend Wedge.
It hasn't been quite what I expected, but I'm very glad we made it this far together.
Go. I'll wait for you here.
Did you find what you seek?
A discoid? Looks like... a compass?
The structure's odd... There might be a hidden compartment inside. Can you try opening it, {PlayerName}?
If this relic was left by the Sentinel, brute force won't get us anywhere.
We'll need a different approach. Maybe a special key, a mechanism, or a password...
Any ideas?
You're right. With the Black Shores' tech, we might be able to outwit our unseen enemy.
Sounds like someone worth meeting.
Do introduce me later. I'm curious what kind of person she is.
But one thing I'm sure of, she must be a formidable opponent and a figure to be respected, like you.
Alright. Let's set all this aside and enjoy the victory we've earned.
There's probably a bonfire roaring at the encampment by now… {PlayerName}, it's time to return to those waiting for us.
We won this battle, but the war is far from over. The road ahead's still steeped in blood and thorns. But with you, and everyone who stood beside us, the banner of Septimont will never fall.
With the eternal sun as my witness, the name of hero belongs to you all.
Warriors, do not let fear stand in our way. The untarnished glory of Septimont stands behind us.
The devastation that befell Murmurstown sixteen years ago will never be repeated.
Because now, we are here.
The Sovereign won't make things easy for us. Any rash action before we can confirm its location and status would be unwise.
Yes. This will trap Sovereign inside the town.
Good. Proceed as planned, Avidius.
Now, then. It falls to us to track down the Sovereign's whereabouts, {PlayerName}. Time is short, so we'll need to split up.
Keep your guard up. Those <te href=750019>Hollow Figures</te>, do not be fooled by their stillness. Those are just the shell. And watch for their songs of corruption.
They were once our own—brave Gladiators, loyal citizens of Septimont.
The Dark Tide not only twisted their minds, it also raised their corpses and turned them into mindless puppets. Not even death could set them free.
Mark my words, it will pay for this malevolence.
That's me.
Heh, guess there's no denying it now. I thought I might keep my foolishness as a child from you.
Yes. It's been a long time. I never imagined I'd see my past like this.
About Manes Deep and the Oak Hollow?
...The name Fabianum once stood for glory.
As Septimont's frontline against the Dark Tide, it held the honor of receiving legends like Fabius, Atilius, and Valeria.
Perhaps that's why Hero of Heroes tales always lingered here. The most popular one was the tale of a great journey to Manes Deep, the ruins of an ancient civilization.
It was said that if you brought "two lives"—yourself and your sword—you'd be granted entry. And within, you'd hear the unceasing whispers of the legendary heroes.
Whispers... they say, capable of awakening a Resonance ability as mighty as the heroes' own.
As for the Oak Hollow said to lead to Manes Deep... just children's tales. No one ever knew if it was real.
But Fabianum never found its Hero of Heroes... Ever since then, the name was lost, replaced by Murmurstown, a scar that Septimont refuses to face...
The whole town held out until the very end, yet no aid ever came from the City of Septimont.
Later, I heard from the senior Gladiators that on the very morning Fabianum fell, most of the Senate lords were still sound asleep in their warm beds...
Heh... it wasn't the Dark Tide that doomed my hometown, but those in power who were blinded by greed and failed to act.
Forgive me. I let my emotions get the better of me.
I see. Then perhaps I should spend more time with you. Like Iuno once did.
Anyway...
Why did the Sonoro reveal all this to us?
A soul who endured the Dark Tide's corruption... and still fights to speak the truth?
Hmm... his spirit deserves to be remembered. I will go to witness that truth with my own eyes.
The Dark Tide paid me no mind. Its target was always Angel.
And the whispers… they were real, but not as the legends promised. What I heard were words of cruelty.
None of it matters now. Those things belong to the past...
I was a reckless child, a hatchling flailing with a sword I could barely hold.
True... though, back then, I didn't really have a choice.
This is Septimont, a place where ideals bow to strength.
As a child, I had no desire for power. But in the City of Septimont, if I wished to rise beyond my origin and silence arrogance dressed in fame and fortune, I had no other path.
Now, my strength speaks in my stead, and my blade cuts down every slander.
All but prejudice.
I know well what those parasites in the Senate are plotting. Draped in the Temple's name, they manipulate public opinion to secure themselves a place on Capitoline Hill.
But their absurd schemes betray only fear—
Too afraid to face me, they skulk in the shadows, hoping empty prophecies might bring me to my knees.
It's not about belief. It's about not fearing them.
The Dark Tide still haunts Septimont. People live in terror of losing their homeland again, and so they cling to the hope of a Hero of Heroes.
As Septimont's Ephor, I must stay strong and answer their call.
A ruler has no right to falter.
If my Resonance ability raises doubts about my right to rule, then let the true Hero of Heroes step forth and challenge me.
And if none appear... I will decide whether this title is worthy of the weight of a ruler's glory.
One of the prophecies speaks of a Hero of Heroes wielding extraordinary power, rising from the dark tides. As I see it, you, {PlayerName}, fit that image well.
Should it be you... I would gladly accept your challenge.
A person of immense strength is either a nemesis or a kindred spirit, both of which are intriguing to me and my blade.
I see...
Iuno once said something similar to me.
...What happened within the Oak Hollow remains a mystery. All I know is, when I found her, her Resonance ability had grown immensely, so powerful that even the Dark Tide could not withstand it.
But she failed to control it. I could do nothing but watch as she transformed into... something no longer human. After that, I never saw her again.
No traces of the Sovereign... It must be hiding again.
We must force it to show itself.
...Over there.
Iuno, I...
No, it can't end like this...
All able units! Split into two teams! One will escort the wounded back to base, while the other prepares supplies and maintains position!
Nothing… Just something on my mind.
Do you still remember what the whispers told me sixteen years ago?
"The Dark Tide answers only to strength. Those without it are doomed to be forsaken..."
Now I think of it, it is rather interesting...
I've never told anyone this before.
But ever since the Dark Tide decided to "forsake" me, those broken whispers have never left.
They echoed in every battle, telling me when to dodge, when to strike. But with each opponent that fell, the whispers began to fade.
Until I became the Ephor...
...That's when they returned. Louder, never giving me a single moment of peace.
I tried to shut them out, but every time I strayed from their guidance, fate would strike me down in the cruelest of ways.
I thought I was the master of my fate, but the truth is... they always knew the path better than I ever could.
...The old prophecy about Ragunna that they once whispered to me has come true.
Now, it is time for me to put their power to the test.
It's the Dark Tide...
The entire frequency range is under its control. In other words, we're completely cut off here.
No!
The Sovereign is far stronger than before, and you've only just recovered. There's no way you can take on that much Resonance energy at once.
Hmm...
Iuno...
Iuno, one last question.
For someone without an unwritten fate... is it true they can never break free, no matter how hard they struggle?
That's alright.
Let's go, {PlayerName}.
My fellow Gladiators! Our prey has finally revealed itself. Move to the marked coordinates. It's time to hunt.
{PlayerName}, you've come.
Bruno has already filled you in on the Rosebearer matter, yes? How are the celebrations treating you?
Iuno?
Everyone has forgotten... No, that's not it. Everyone says they've never even seen her before.
But you, you remember her. You remember everything that happened. You are certain she is real and cannot exist in your memories alone.
I know what you're feeling, because I've felt it too.
The Dark Tide's frequencies twisted my perception as well. Made me see things that weren't real.
The Sovereign's whispers dogged me for decades, tempting me to accept its gift.
But that's all in the past. I know now that to find peace, I must see through the deception, fight it, and conquer it.
Because of my sword. It reflects everything that happened in Murmurstown.
Existence always leaves a trace. Beyond feeling, memory, and perception, is there anything that can prove this "Iuno" isn't an illusion?
That's enough. I allow requests... but only three.
Quite the unique design. Like a crescent moon. But still... I'm sorry.
I wish I could offer you the answer you seek, but to me, it's just an ordinary bracelet. Nothing about it stands out.
When you mentioned her name, I tried to recall any trace of her in my memories.
But there is nothing. What has passed is clear... and she feels like an extra piece to a puzzle, one that doesn't quite fit.
{PlayerName}, wait...
What... did you see?
